summary: EC2 instance batch-worker-17 (i-0b9c8d7e6f5a41203), a production c5.4xlarge in the data-eng fleet, made 3,117 UDP DNS queries over approximately three hours to pool.minexmr.example, a known XMR cryptocurrency mining pool confirmed on GuardDuty's threat intel list. CloudTrail independently corroborates compromise: the instance profile attempted an anomalous DescribeInstances enumeration call (denied) and a successful GetAuthorizationToken call to ECR, consistent with pulling a mining container image — neither action is expected from a batch worker. Two proportionate containment actions (security-group isolation and IAM session revocation) have been proposed and are pending human approval; the data-eng team should be consulted to rule out any sanctioned mining workload before execution.

evidence.observation: GuardDuty type CryptoCurrency:EC2/BitcoinTool.B!DNS, severity 8.0. Instance i-0b9c8d7e6f5a41203 made 3,117 DNS queries to pool.minexmr.example (UDP, not blocked) between 2026-07-12T22:03Z and 2026-07-13T01:17Z. Domain confirmed on ExampleThreatIntelSet threat list.

Within the incident window, batch-worker-profile made a failed DescribeInstances call (Client.UnauthorizedOperation — anomalous enumeration probe) and a successful ECR GetAuthorizationToken call from 10.20.4.19, consistent with pulling a mining container. No legitimate batch-job API calls were observed in the 72-hour window.

Public IP 192.0.2.201 (the instance's outbound egress IP) has no abuse reports in the last 90 days and is unknown to threat intel. This is expected for the victim's own egress IP and does not weaken the finding.

proposed_actions.justification: The instance made 3,117 DNS queries over ~3 hours to pool.minexmr.example, a known XMR mining pool domain on GuardDuty's threat intel list. Isolating the security group cuts off the mining pool connection and any C2 channel while preserving the instance for forensic analysis.
proposed_actions.risk_if_wrong: If this is an authorised cryptocurrency workload, isolating the instance will interrupt production data-engineering batch jobs. The data-eng team (tag: Team=data-eng) should be notified immediately and asked to confirm whether any mining workload is sanctioned.

proposed_actions.justification: CloudTrail shows the instance profile made an anomalous failed DescribeInstances call (access denied — likely an enumeration or privilege escalation probe) and a GetAuthorizationToken call to ECR (potentially pulling a mining container image). Revoking active sessions prevents further lateral movement or data exfiltration using the compromised instance's credentials.
proposed_actions.risk_if_wrong: Revoking sessions will interrupt any legitimate batch jobs currently using this instance profile across the fleet. Scope should be confirmed against the batch-worker-17 instance specifically before applying fleet-wide; consider scoping revocation to the single instance's temporary credentials if tooling permits.
